title: About
text: Hi! I’m Ann Carlson. I am a sophomore sports media and broadcasting major. Here is a blog about the Husker basketball 2026 historic season.

title: Nebraska basketball had their most historic season
text:
Did Nebraska have their most historical season because of their offense?
Nebraska basketball has had the most historic season ever. They made it to the Sweet 16, won 24 games in a row and were ranked in the top 10. During the 2025 season they didn’t even make it to the NCAA Tournament.
Anyone watching Nebraska basketball this season could see there was a difference in Husker offense, specifically 3-pointers. Pryce Sandfort transferred to Nebraska putting up 129 3-pointers which was very helpful for the Huskers numbers. But it wasn’t just the threes that helped the Huskers during this historic season. Their overall shooting percentage and offensive rebounds also improved greatly.
Let’s look at some numbers.
We are going to look at data from the 2025 and 2026 season that was created by Sports References.
To see how Nebraska has scored more 3-pointers this year compared to last year we will look at the 3-point percentage. This will also be compared to other Big Ten schools by using a slope chart.


Code
library(tidyverse)
library(ggrepel)

logs26 <- read_csv("https://mattwaite.github.io/sportsdatafiles/logs26.csv")
logs25 <- read_csv("https://mattwaite.github.io/sportsdatafiles/logs25.csv")


threes26 <- logs26 |>
group_by(team, conference, season) |>
  summarise(
    total3P = sum(team3p), 
    total3PA = sum(team3pa)
  ) |>
  mutate(season3ppct = total3P/total3PA)

threes25 <- logs25 |>
group_by(TeamFull, Conference, Season) |>
  summarise(
    total3P = sum(Team3P), 
    total3PA = sum(Team3PA)
  ) |>
  mutate(season3ppct = total3P/total3PA) |> 
  rename(
    team = TeamFull,
    conference = Conference,
    season = Season
  )

threes <- bind_rows(threes25, threes26)

threes$season <- factor(threes$season, levels=c("2024-2025", "2025-2026"))

bigthrees <- threes |> filter(conference == "Big Ten")

nu <- bigthrees |> filter(team == "Nebraska Cornhuskers")

iu <- bigthrees |> filter(team == "Iowa Hawkeyes")

mu <- bigthrees |> filter(team == "Michigan Wolverines")

ggplot() + 
  geom_line(data=bigthrees, aes(x=season, y=season3ppct, group=team), color="grey") +
  geom_point(data=bigthrees, aes(x=season, y=season3ppct), color="grey") + 
  geom_line(data=nu, aes(x=season, y=season3ppct, group=team), color="#e41c38") +
  geom_point(data=nu, aes(x=season, y=season3ppct, group=team), color="#e41c38") +
  geom_line(data=iu, aes(x=season, y=season3ppct, group=team), color="#FFCD00") +
  geom_point(data=iu, aes(x=season, y=season3ppct, group=team), color="#FFCD00") +
  geom_line(data=mu, aes(x=season, y=season3ppct, group=team), color="#00274C") +
  geom_point(data=mu, aes(x=season, y=season3ppct, group=team), color="#00274C") +
  geom_text_repel(data=bigthrees |> filter(season == "2024-2025"), aes(x=season, y=season3ppct, group=team, label=team), direction="y", nudge_x = -1, hjust=1) +
  geom_text_repel(data=bigthrees |> filter(season == "2025-2026"), aes(x=season, y=season3ppct, group=team, label=team), direction="y", nudge_x = 1, hjust=1) +
  #geom_text(data=bigthrees |> filter(season == min(season)), aes(x=season, y=season3ppct, group=team, label=team)) +
  #scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(2025, 2026), limits=c(2025,2026))  +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x="season", 
    y="season 3-point shooting", 
    title="The Huskers had a high jump in 3-point averages", 
    subtitle="They went from near the lower-middle section of being average to the upper half, just above Iowa.", 
    caption="Source: Sports References | By Ann Carlson"
    ) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title.position = "plot"
  )









Nebraska has moved from the lower-middle section to the upper half, putting them near some of the top teams in the Big Ten. This means that in the 2026 season they were making far more 3-pointers than in the 2025 season. The closest in competition would be the Iowa Hawkeyes who Nebraska lost to in the Sweet 16. Also included was the Michigan Wolverines who Nebraska lost to by three in the regular season. They won the entire NCAA Tournament.
But what about all teams in college basketball? Where does Nebraska rank out against other teams in different conferences as far as shooting percentage?


Code
seasonshooting <- logs25 |>
group_by(TeamFull, Conference, Season) |>
  summarise(
    seasonteamfg = sum(TeamFG, na.rm=TRUE), 
    seasonteamfga = sum(TeamFGA, na.rm=TRUE),
    seasonopponentfg= sum(OpponentFG, na.rm=TRUE),
    seasonopponentfga= sum(OpponentFGA, na.rm=TRUE),
  ) |>
  mutate(
    seasonteamfgpct = seasonteamfg/seasonteamfga,
    seasonopponentfgpct = seasonopponentfg/seasonopponentfga, 
      ) |> ungroup()


nu <- seasonshooting |> filter(TeamFull=="Nebraska Cornhuskers")
mu <- seasonshooting |> filter(TeamFull=="Michigan Wolverines")
iu <- seasonshooting |> filter(TeamFull=="Illinois Fighting Illini")
au <- seasonshooting |> filter(TeamFull=="Arizona Wildcats")
uconn <- seasonshooting |> filter(TeamFull=="UConn Huskies")

ggplot() + 
  geom_point(data=seasonshooting, aes(x=seasonteamfgpct, y=seasonopponentfgpct), 
    color="grey", 
    alpha=.3) +
   geom_point(data=nu, aes(x=seasonteamfgpct, y=seasonopponentfgpct), 
    color="red") +
  geom_point(data=mu, aes(x=seasonteamfgpct, y=seasonopponentfgpct), 
    color="blue") +
  geom_point(data=iu, aes(x=seasonteamfgpct, y=seasonopponentfgpct), 
    color="orange") +
  geom_point(data=au, aes(x=seasonteamfgpct, y=seasonopponentfgpct), 
    color="#0C234B") +
  geom_point(data=uconn, aes(x=seasonteamfgpct, y=seasonopponentfgpct), 
    color="green") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.4480238) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.4358616) + 
  labs(
    x="Team shooting", 
    y="Opponent shooting", 
    title="Nebraska is in the upper half of shooting percentage", 
    subtitle="The Husker’s finished in the same quarter as national champions Michigan and some Final Four teams.", 
    caption="Source: Sports References | By Ann Carlson"
    ) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title.position = "plot"
    )









Nebraska also saw an increase in offensive rebounds from the 2025 season, but they still didn’t look good compared to the rest of the Big Ten. How much better were they than last year? This dumbbell chart shows how Nebraska matches up against other Big Ten schools with offensive rebounds.


Code
big_ten_rebounds <- logs26 %>%
  filter(conference == "Big Ten") %>%
  group_by(team) %>%
  summarize(
    TeamOffensiveRebounds = sum(team_orb, na.rm = TRUE),
    TeamTotalRebounds = sum(team_trb, na.rm = TRUE)
      ) %>%
  mutate(TeamDefensiveRebounds = TeamTotalRebounds-TeamOffensiveRebounds)

nureb <- big_ten_rebounds %>%
  filter(team == "Nebraska Cornhuskers") 



ggplot() + 
  geom_segment(data=big_ten_rebounds, aes(x = TeamOffensiveRebounds, xend = TeamDefensiveRebounds, y = reorder(team, TeamTotalRebounds), yend = reorder(team, TeamTotalRebounds)), color = "grey") +
  geom_point(data=big_ten_rebounds, aes(x = TeamOffensiveRebounds, y = reorder(team, TeamTotalRebounds)), color = "red") +
  geom_point(data=big_ten_rebounds, aes(x = TeamDefensiveRebounds, y = reorder(team, TeamTotalRebounds)), color = "black") +
  
  geom_segment(data=nureb, aes(x = TeamOffensiveRebounds, xend = TeamDefensiveRebounds, y = reorder(team, TeamTotalRebounds), yend = reorder(team, TeamTotalRebounds)), color = "red", linewidth = 2) +
  geom_point(data=nureb, aes(x = TeamOffensiveRebounds, y = reorder(team, TeamTotalRebounds)), color = "red", size=2) +
  geom_point(data=nureb, aes(x = TeamDefensiveRebounds, y = reorder(team, TeamTotalRebounds)), color = "black", size=2) + 
  labs(
    x="Team Offensive Rebounds", 
    y="", 
    title="Nebraska offensive rebound is better than last year", 
    subtitle="Nebraska averages around 850 offensive rebounds where the best team in the Big Ten is near 1000. 
In 2025 they were at 250.", 
    caption="Source: Data Provider | By Ann Carlson"
    ) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title.position = "plot"
    )









Nebraska has a solid group of juniors and sophomores coming back next year. Assuming key players like Pryce Sandfort don’t enter the transfer portal they have a good chance of once again making history.
Was this historic finish because Nebraska upped their offensive game, especially in 3-pointers? The answer is yes and now the question is will they continue to dominate in the Big Ten next year and have another historic season.
